LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER CAROLINE LEE'S HEARING ON PETITION FOR LEAVE TO FILE AGAINST GOVERNMENTAL ENTITY IS GRANTED. PETITIONER'S FAILURE TO FILE A CLAIM WITHIN THE STATUTORY PERIOD WAS DUE TO EXCUSABLE NEGLECT AND MISTAKE. FURTHER, RESPONDENT IS NOT PREJUDICED AS THIRD PARTIES HAVE ALREADY FILED SUIT REGARDING THE UNDERLYING ACCIDENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against Governmental Entity
Off Calendar Aug-27-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KAREN PLATT OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BORIS EFRON, TEL. NO. (650) 851-8880 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER CAROLINE LEE'S Hearing On Petition For Leave To File Against Governmental Entity is Granted. Petitioner's failure to file a claim within the statutory period was due to excusable neglect and mistake. Further, Respondent is not prejudiced as third parties have already filed suit regarding the underlying accident. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =(302/MJM)
